explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: Filings state the inability to successfully acquire, develop, implement, or update AI technology as a risk factor, describing its potential impacts and outlining strategic consideration for managing AI technology risks. Press releases describe the achievement of automated metadata governance for AI accelerators and reference an AI-first system deployment, stating it implies governance considerations. Court filings also reference the development and implementation of an AI chatbot, describing it as a governance mechanism for AI deployment, with press releases stating the critical need for data quality for accurate algorithm performance.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: The 2024 Form 10-K filing by CSX identifies the inability to successfully acquire, develop, implement, or update new or existing technology, including artificial intelligence, as a risk factor. This filing provides evidence for the **governance** pillar by discussing potential impacts of AI on the company's competitive position, indicating strategic consideration and future governance needs for managing AI technology risks.
relevant_sources.title: 2024 Form 10-K

relevant_sources.summary: This press release provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. It highlights the achievement of automated metadata governance for AI accelerators and mentions an AI-first system deployment, implying considerations for transparency and governance. Furthermore, the emphasis on AI-driven decision-making and the critical need for data quality for accurate algorithm performance suggest the importance of these responsible AI principles.
relevant_sources.title: CSX Modernizes Data Management System

relevant_sources.summary: This court filing provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. It mentions the development and implementation of an AI chatbot, demonstrating a governance mechanism for AI deployment. Furthermore, the chatbot's use for streamlining the broadband installation permitting process highlights transparency in its application.
relevant_sources.title: Norfolk Southern and CSX sue Cox Communications over broadband installations

oversight.path_to_improvement: Document human review processes for AI-assisted decisions (built or vendor AI).

privacy.path_to_improvement: Document data protection practices for AI systems, including vendor AI data handling.

transparency.findings: CSX describes the use of an AI chatbot for streamlining the broadband installation permitting process, stating this application highlights transparency.
transparency.path_to_improvement: Publish detailed documentation (model cards, system specs) for AI systems deployed.

It highlights the achievement of automated metadata governance for AI accelerators and mentions an AI-first system deployment, implying considerations for transparency and governance. Furthermore, the emphasis on AI-driven decision-making and the critical need for data quality for accurate algorithm performance suggest the importance of these responsible AI principles.

relevant_sources.summary: This court filing provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. It mentions the development and implementation of an AI chatbot, demonstrating a governance mechanism for AI deployment. Furthermore, the chatbot's use for streamlining the broadband installation permitting process highlights transparency in its application.

summary.overall_findings: CSX's court filings describe the use of an AI chatbot for streamlining the broadband installation permitting process, an application highlighted in relation to transparency. Further disclosures address governance, outlining strategic considerations for managing AI technology risks and noting the inability to successfully implement new AI as a risk factor. These two areas represent 2 of 7 responsible AI pillars with documented evidence. Based on a review of 9 publicly available sources, no qualifying public evidence was found for the majority of the 7 evaluated pillars.
